footer.stack: FilaMind Flow — Built by Egyptian makers, for world makers. Happy printing

home.body: Your panels live in the sidebar — pick one to get started. This home is intentionally empty for now.
home.title: Mission Control
home.doctorBody: One read-only scan across pins, driver values, config drift, firmware sync and more — graded A-F with jump-to-fix links.
home.noTuning: No tuning runs archived yet — run the Input Shaping wizard.
home.noBaseline: No hardware baseline saved yet — save a snapshot in the Machine Map to detect changes.
home.hwUnchanged: No hardware changes since the saved baseline.
home.hwChanged: {n} hardware change(s) since the saved baseline.

setup.title: Get started ({done}/{total})
setup.boards: Confirm your boards in the Machine Map
setup.baseline: Save a hardware baseline (detects future changes)
setup.firmware: Bring every MCU firmware in sync with the host
setup.motors: Assign your motors in Motor Drivers
setup.tuning: Tune input shaping for X and Y (guided wizard)

journal.title: Recent activity
journal.empty: Nothing recorded yet — flashes, config saves and tuning runs land here.

firmware-upgrade.title: Firmware Manager
firmware-upgrade.description: MCU firmware versions, host sync status, and toolchain readiness.

input-shaping.title: Input Shaping
input-shaping.description: Analyze a resonance CSV and find the best input shaper.

motor-drivers.title: Motor Drivers
motor-drivers.description: Live TMC stepper-driver inventory: current, mode, microsteps, and status.

config-editor.title: Config Editor
config-editor.description: Browse your printer's config files, parsed into sections with validation.

max-flow.title: Max-Flow
max-flow.description: Measure the highest volumetric flow your hotend can sustain (StallGuard).

board-topology.title: Board Topology
board-topology.description: See how the host and MCUs connect (USB / CAN / UART) + a board guess.

macro-designer.title: Macro Designer
macro-designer.description: Simulate G-code offline — toolhead path, bounds, totals, and a macro library.

hardware-browser.title: Hardware Browser
hardware-browser.description: Search a curated 3D-printing hardware reference by name, manufacturer, or spec.

config-templates.title: Config Templates
config-templates.description: Ready-to-paste Klipper config blocks and macros, by category.

machine-doctor.title: Machine Doctor
machine-doctor.description: One-click full-printer scan with an A-F report card and jump-to-fix links.

host-control.title: Host Control
host-control.description: Manage the printer host's Linux OS: health, services, disk cleanup, and system settings.

klipperscreen-studio.title: KlipperScreen Studio
klipperscreen-studio.description: Edit your printer's KlipperScreen touchscreen config and restart it to apply.

mission.body: FilaMind Flow brings together the best tools for 3D printing — from first setup to mastery — so makers at every level can diagnose, fix, and print better. The project is under active development and research, and new practical ideas are always welcome. Contributions are open to everyone.
mission.tagline: Built by Egyptian makers, for world makers. Happy printing.

dialog.introBug: Describe what went wrong. We'll open a pre-filled issue on GitHub for you to review and submit — nothing is sent automatically.
dialog.introFeature: Describe the feature you'd like. We'll open a pre-filled issue on GitHub for you to review and submit.
dialog.descPlaceholderBug: What did you do, and what happened instead?
dialog.descPlaceholderFeature: What would you like to be able to do?
dialog.attachHint: A picture of the current screen helps us understand the problem.
dialog.noScreenshot: Couldn't capture a screenshot — you can attach one manually.
dialog.includesNote: These details are added to the issue to help with diagnosis. Review them on GitHub before submitting.
dialog.languageNote: The issue is written in English so maintainers can act on it.

sent.title: Issue opened in a new tab
sent.body: Review the pre-filled details on GitHub, then submit the issue there.
sent.copied: Screenshot copied — paste it into the issue with Ctrl/⌘+V.
sent.saved: Screenshot saved to your downloads — drag it into the issue.
sent.failed: Couldn't attach the screenshot automatically — you can add one on GitHub.
